data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify employs a microservices architecture to improve scalability and manageability.
data.technical_spec: Hundreds of interdependent services interface with decentralized databases, ensuring minimal downtime and better resource allocation.

data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Utilization of distributed systems to handle large data and requests.
data.technical_spec: Distributes workloads across multiple servers using Kubernetes for orchestration.

data.title: Event Sourcing
data.description: Spotify uses event sourcing for real-time data processing and fault tolerance.
data.technical_spec: Kafka is majorly used along with a combination of logs and event processing systems.

data.title: APIs and SDKs
data.description: Robust set of APIs and SDKs for developers to build Spotify-integrated applications.
data.technical_spec: Offers RESTful APIs with OAuth 2.0 for secure access and a variety of SDKs for different platforms.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Globally distributed CDN to improve user experience and reduce latency.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Akamai and other CDN providers to deliver content efficiently.
